title: tephrosia barclayi
intro: Tephrosia barclayi, also known as Barclay's horsefly bush or horsefly weed, is a plant species within the legume family. Traditionally, it has been used in various forms due to its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities. The health benefits associated with Tephrosia barclayi include its potential to treat conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations. Herbal preparations of Tephrosia barclayi often involve infusions or decoctions of its leaves and stems, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
